D5 Design L3 Apply Atom

Pantone Spot Colours

a brand-red that no CMYK mix can hit — browse the PMS system and why spot inks exist alongside process colour

The Pantone Matching System names pre-mixed spot inks, each a physical recipe rather than a screen colour. Brands rely on them because a spot ink reproduces identically on every press, and many — bright oranges, deep blues, neons — sit outside the CMYK process gamut entirely. On a screen we can only approximate them.

Step through a sampler of well-known PMS colours. The large swatch is an approximate sRGB rendering; the strip shows neighbours. Remember the on-screen value only stands in for a physical ink chip.
